data.title: American Financial declares $2.00 special dividend
data.description:
• American Financial (NYSE:AFG) declares $2.00/share special dividend.
  • Payable March 28; for shareholders of record March 17; ex-div March 17.
  • See AFG Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: FuboTV stock slumps after forecast fails to impress, posts mixed Q4
data.description:
Shares of FuboTV (NYSE:FUBO) were down nearly 14% in Friday's premarket session after the TV streaming platform forecast below Wall Street expectations for the current quarter and reported a mixed fourth quarter.

The company expects Q1 revenue in North America to be $400M to $410M ($405M midpoint) and from the rest of the world to be $7.5M to $8.5M ($8M midpoint). When combining both segments, total revenue is forecast to be between $407.5M and $418.5M ($413M midpoint) according to Seeking Alpha's calculations, which is below the consensus estimate of $436.9M.

Fubo also expects to have 1.430M to 1.460M total subscribers in North America and 330,000 to 340,000 subscribers in ROW.

In Q4, the company saw a 4% subscriber growth in North America at 1.676M, while average revenue per user was $87.90. For ROW, paid subscribers declined nearly 11% to 362,000 and ARPU was $8.50. The consensus estimates for ARPU are $87.90 and $8.90, respectively.

Net loss from continuing operations for the three months ended December 31 narrowed to $40.9M, or a loss per share of $0.11, compared to a loss of $84.4M, or a $0.24 loss per share, in the same period last year.

On an adjusted basis, the company lost 2 cents, while analysts were expecting it to lose 11 cents apiece.

Revenue rose 7.8% to $431.8M and missed the consensus estimate of $445.2M.

data.title: NextTrip converts $2.6M in short-term debt to restricted equity
data.description:
• NextTrip (NASDAQ:NTRP) announced on Friday the conversion of $2.6M in short-term debt, including $1.5M previously advanced by the company's Chairman and CEO.
  • These loans were converted into restricted equity ahead of completion of the company's fiscal year-ended February 28, 2025.
  • Additionally, certain other creditors agreed to convert their $1.1 million of debt into restricted equity. This is in addition to the $1.75 million in short-term promissory notes previously converted to restricted equity on December 31, 2024.
  • These strategic moves eliminate most of the debt from NextTrip's balance sheet, replacing cash repayment obligations, including for accrued principal and interest, with the issuance of restricted shares of the company's stock.
  • This significantly enhances the company's balance sheet and frees up capital to drive key initiatives.

data.title: MacKenzie Realty Capital announces ~$4.8M registered direct offering
data.description:
• Commercial and residential assets-focused REIT MacKenzie Realty Capital (NASDAQ:MKZR) announced on Friday an ~$4.8M registered direct offering and a concurrent private placement.
  • The company entered into a securities purchase agreement with a single institutional investor for the purchase and sale of 2.83M shares of common stock.
  • Concurrently, MacKenzie also agreed to issue unregistered Series A common warrants to purchase up to 1.41M shares and Series B common warrants to purchase up to 2.83M shares.
  • The combined effective offering price for each share and accompanying warrant is $1.71.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about March 3.
  • The net proceeds are intended to be used for capital expenditures, including acquisitions of additional properties, strategic principal payments on outstanding loans, payment of operating expenses, fees and other costs, funding for their preferred stock share repurchase program, other sales and marketing activities and working capital and general corporate purposes.

data.title: CAVA Group is recommended at Piper Sandler after recent stumble
data.description:
Piper Sandler upgraded CAVA Group (NYSE:CAVA) to an Overweight rating on Friday after having the restaurant stock slotted at Neutral.

Analyst Brian Mullan and his team see CAVA (NYSE:CAVA) as one of the best ways to invest in the secular growth of fast casual, especially amid an environment that is choppy and with CAVA shares down 20% year-to-date.

"We think that CAVA is one of the best positioned companies in our coverage to navigate anyenvironment. Specifically, we note that CAVA has taken only ~15% menu pricing versus2019 (as compared to FAFH at ~30%, and more at certain restaurant peers); and whilethis is likely not the only reason for CAVA's traffic outperformance, it has almost certainlyhelped."

For Q4, CAVA Group (CAVA) reported revenue increased 28.3% to $225.1 million. The increase was driven by 77 net new CAVA restaurant openings during the quarter and same-restaurant sales growth of 21.2%, including traffic growth of 15.6%. CAVA's restaurant-level profit margin improved 50 basis points from a year ago to 22.4% of sales.

Shares of CAVA Group (CAVA) were up 1.1% in early trading on Friday.

data.title: Armlogi announces increase in its small parcel shipping volume following Temu marketplace integration
data.description:
• Armlogi (NASDAQ:BTOC) announced that small parcel shipping increased from 128,744 parcels in November 2024 to 167,298 in December 2024, representing 30% growth over such period.
  • In January 2025, volumes reached 194,304 parcels, marking a further 16% increase.
  • Armlogi now serves over 200 Temu customers, a 29% increase from the 155 customers reported in October 2024.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: HP falls after weak Q2 outlook, analysts see more reliance on second half fiscal 2025
data.description:
HP Inc. (NYSE:HPQ) stock fell about 5% premarket on Friday after its fiscal first quarter 2025 results and a lower than expected second quarter EPS outlook, which saw mixed reactions from analysts.

Evercore maintained its Outperform rating and $40 price target on Dell's stock.

Analysts led by Amit Daryanani said HP delivered an in-line quarterly and maintained its fiscal 2025 guidance. The Personal Systems, or PS, business grew revenue 5%, with solid 10% year-over-year growth in commercial PS partially offset by a 7% decline in consumer (notably their performance continue to indicate share gains).

The company noted double-digit growth in commercial personal computer, or PC, revenue driven by the Windows 11 refresh and growing demand for AI PCs. Printing revenue was down 1% in constant currency and operating margin declined by 90bps year-over-year, although they remain at the high-end of the long-term target of 16% to 19%, the analysts added.

The analysts noted that current tariffs on China are a headwind which is incorporated in the fiscal 2025 outlook. HP has been proactive around building inventory ahead of the tariffs, which will help mitigate the impact, but does represent a near-term free cash flow, or FCF, headwind (Q2 FCFexpected to be negative). HP has also made supply chain changes and plans to have only 10% of U.S. revenue sourced from China by the end of fiscal 2025.

Daryanani and his team added that annualized savings from the Future Ready plan were increased by $300M and HP now expects $1.9B in total savings by the end of fiscal 2025. This will help offset some of the impact from tariffs.

Broadly, EPS & FCF continue to remain fairly stable and should provide a solid support around low $30 for the stock, according to the analysts. The analysts noted that HP continues to do well in navigating a challenging geopolitical environment and they think the share gains could sustain both in PCs and potentially print over time.

Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley kept its Equal-weight rating but lowered the price target to $35 from $36.

Analyst Erik Woodring and his team said that the first quarter results were in-line with expectations but HP's management guided fiscal second quarter EPS $0.05 below Morgan Stanley/Street estimates.

The analysts noted that the fiscal second quarter guidance also implies fiscal first half EPS representing just 43% of annual EPS, a 10-year low, pointing to a steep and ambitious profitability ramp in the second half of the fiscal year.

The analysts added that stronger PCs and new cost cuts should help, yet amid the many market uncertainties, this guidance feels ambitious.

Related stocks: Dell (DELL), which also reported its quarterly results, saw its stock dip about 7% premarket on Friday.

data.title: Novartis gets EU positive recommendation for its kidney disease treatment
data.description:
Novartis (NYSE:NVS) announced Friday that the European Medicines Agency (EMA), endorsed Fabhalta for the treatment of adults with C3 glomerulopathy.

Issuing a positive opinion, EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) has recommended the marketing authorization for Fabhalta to treat C3 glomerulopathy, a rare kidney disease.The CHMP recommendation will next be reviewed by the European Commission (EC) before a final decision on marketing authorization which is expected within two months.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: FUBO, ADSK, META and LLY
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures were largely higher on Friday, as market participants received a key inflation reading that came in-line with expectations. Here are some stocks to watch on Friday:

  • Shares of FuboTV (NYSE:FUBO) gave up earlier gains to slip nearly 9% in pre-market trading. The live sports streaming platform delivered a mixed Q4 report, missing on the top-line but beating bottom-line expectations. FUBO said Q4 marked its first quarter of positive free cash flow. The company ended full-year 2024 with 1.676M paid subscribers in North America. For Q1 2025, FuboTV (NYSE:FUBO) sees North American revenue of $400M to $410M and subscribers of 1.43M to 1.46M. FUBO in January said Disney's (DIS) Hulu + Live TV business will merge with Fubo.
  • Autodesk (NASDAQ:ADSK) stock added about 1% ahead of the opening bell, after the software firm said it would lay off 1,350 employees as part of a worldwide restructuring plan. It also delivered a FQ4 2025 top- and bottom-line beat, with total quarterly billings jumping 23% to $2.11B. Autodesk (NASDAQ:ADSK) software is used in the construction industry, by design engineers and by media professionals for animation and visual effects. Top boss Andrew Anagnost on the earnings conference call said the restructuring plan will enable ADSK "to deliver GAAP margins among the best in the industry."
  • Class A shares of Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) were in focus, up slightly ahead of market open. A CNBC report said the Facebook-parent intended to release its own artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot app in an effort to compete with the likes of OpenAI's ChatGPT and Google's Gemini. The app is expected to debut in the second quarter, CNBC said, citing people familiar with the matter. Meta (NASDAQ:META) has earlier announced plans to invest as much as $65B on AI projects in 2025, and this move would be more evidence of its AI ambitions.
  • Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) stock was down marginally in pre-market trading, but for the week it was up 3.6%. It has been a busy few days for the world's largest pharmaceutical firm. The company on Tuesday announced the launch of additional vial doses of its blockbuster obesity drug Zepbound. On the same day, it separately said it would acquire biotech Organovo's (ONVO) investigational ulcerative colitis drug. Then on Wednesday, Lilly (NYSE:LLY) unveiled plans to build four new pharmaceutical manufacturing sites in the U.S., bringing its total investment in the country to more than $50B since 2020.

data.title: NuRAN receives second drawdown of $1.05M from Cygnum Capital
data.description:
• NuRAN Wireless (OTCQB:NRRWF) has received approval for the second drawdown of $1.05M from the Facility for Energy Inclusion, a fund managed by Cygnum Capital.
  • This follows the first drawdown of $2.5M received on July 16, 2024, as part of the $5M loan facility agreement announced on April 26, 2024.
  • NuRAN has chosen to dedicate more resources to the region, reinforcing its commitment to accelerate deployments and strengthen its financial performance under the objective of achieveing EBITDA positive as soon as possible.
  • "The receipt of this second drawdown is a testament to the strong partnership between NuRAN and FEI as we continue our mission to expand mobile connectivity in remote areas across Africa. These funds will help us achieve further significant progress towards our 2025 objectives." said Francis Létourneau, CEO of NuRAN Wireless.
